exuberant.en: full of energy, high spirits, and enthusiasm

ex.en: She gave an exuberant welcome to the guests.
ex.ko: 그녀는 손님들을 활기차게 반갑게 맞이했다.

exuberant.en: (of plants) growing thickly and healthily in great abundance

ex.en: Exuberant vines covered the old garden wall.
ex.ko: 무성한 덩굴이 오래된 정원 담을 뒤덮었다.

exuberantly.en: in a way that shows great energy, enthusiasm, or happiness

ex.en: The crowd cheered exuberantly when the team won.
ex.ko: 팀이 이겼을 때 관중들은 열정적으로 환호했다.

ex.en: The family is fabulously wealthy.
ex.ko: 그 가족은 엄청나게 부유하다.

ex.en: He has a distinctive facial expression when he's thinking.
ex.ko: 그는 생각할 때 독특한 얼굴 표정을 짓는다.

facial.en: a beauty treatment for the face involving cleansing and massage

ex.en: She treated herself to a facial at the spa.
ex.ko: 그녀는 스파에서 얼굴 마사지를 받으며 자신에게 선물을 주었다.

facilitation.en: the act of making a process or activity easier or helping it progress smoothly

ex.en: The workshop focused on the facilitation of team communication.
ex.ko: 그 워크숍은 팀 소통 촉진에 초점을 맞췄다.

faction.en: a smaller group within a larger organization or party that has different opinions from the rest

ex.en: The party split into rival factions over the new policy.
ex.ko: 그 정당은 새 정책을 둘러싸고 대립하는 파벌로 갈라졌다.

fanatic.en: a person with extreme and uncritical enthusiasm for something

ex.en: He's a total fitness fanatic.
ex.ko: 그는 완전히 헬스광이다.

fanatic.en: showing extreme and uncritical enthusiasm or devotion

ex.en: Her fanatic devotion to the cause surprised everyone.
ex.ko: 그 대의에 대한 그녀의 광신적인 헌신은 모두를 놀라게 했다.

fanatically.en: in a way that shows extreme and uncritical enthusiasm

ex.en: He supports his team fanatically.
ex.ko: 그는 광적으로 자신의 팀을 응원한다.

fanciful.en: based on imagination rather than fact or reason

ex.en: The plan was dismissed as fanciful and impractical.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 비현실적이고 실현 불가능하다고 여겨져 기각되었다.

fanciful.en: elaborately or curiously decorated, showing a lot of imagination

ex.en: The building has a fanciful facade with intricate carvings.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 정교한 조각이 있는 기발한 외관을 하고 있다.

farce.en: a ridiculous and absurd situation, especially one that is meant to be taken seriously

ex.en: The trial turned into a complete farce.
ex.ko: 그 재판은 완전히 웃음거리가 되어버렸다.

farce.en: a comic play full of ridiculous and exaggerated situations

ex.en: The theater is putting on a classic French farce.
ex.ko: 그 극장은 고전 프랑스 소극을 공연하고 있다.

fatality.en: a death caused by an accident, disaster, or violence

ex.en: The car crash resulted in three fatalities.
ex.ko: 그 자동차 사고로 세 명이 사망했다.

fatality.en: the quality of being likely to cause death

ex.en: The fatality rate of the disease has dropped sharply.
ex.ko: 그 질병의 치사율이 급격히 떨어졌다.

fatigue.en: extreme tiredness resulting from hard physical or mental effort

ex.en: She felt a wave of fatigue after the long flight.
ex.ko: 그녀는 긴 비행 후 극심한 피로를 느꼈다.

ex.en: The long hike fatigued the entire group.
ex.ko: 그 긴 등산은 일행 전체를 지치게 했다.

fatigue.cue: 금속 등 재료가 반복된 스트레스로 약해짐
fatigue.en: weakening of a material caused by repeated stress over time

ex.en: Metal fatigue was the cause of the bridge's collapse.
ex.ko: 금속 피로가 그 다리 붕괴의 원인이었다.

favorably.en: in a way that shows approval or a positive opinion

ex.en: The critics reviewed the film favorably.
ex.ko: 평론가들은 그 영화를 호의적으로 평가했다.

ex.en: The ferocious storm destroyed several houses.
ex.ko: 그 사나운 폭풍이 집 여러 채를 파괴했다.

ferocious.en: very intense in effort, determination, or criticism

ex.en: Competition among the top firms is ferocious.
ex.ko: 최고 기업들 간의 경쟁은 매우 치열하다.

ferociously.en: in an extremely fierce or violent manner

ex.en: The dog barked ferociously at the stranger.
ex.ko: 그 개는 낯선 사람에게 사납게 짖었다.

fiddle.en: an informal word for a violin, especially in folk music

ex.en: He picked up his fiddle and started playing a folk tune.
ex.ko: 그는 바이올린을 집어 들고 민요를 연주하기 시작했다.

fiddle.en: touch or move something repeatedly with your hands, often out of nervousness

ex.en: She fiddled with her pen during the meeting.
ex.ko: 그녀는 회의 중에 펜을 만지작거렸다.

fiddle.en: change something dishonestly in order to gain an advantage, especially financial records

ex.en: He was accused of fiddling the accounts.
ex.ko: 그는 장부를 조작한 혐의를 받았다.

fiddler.en: a person who plays the violin, especially folk music

ex.en: The fiddler played a lively tune at the wedding.
ex.ko: 그 바이올린 연주자는 결혼식에서 흥겨운 곡을 연주했다.

fiddly.en: difficult to do or handle because it involves small or delicate parts

ex.en: Assembling the model was a fiddly task.
ex.ko: 그 모형을 조립하는 일은 손이 많이 가는 까다로운 작업이었다.

ex.en: The tabloids called the killer a fiend.
ex.ko: 타블로이드지는 그 살인범을 악마 같은 자라고 불렀다.

fiend.en: (informal) a person who is very enthusiastic about something

ex.en: My roommate is a total coffee fiend.
ex.ko: 내 룸메이트는 완전히 커피광이다.

ex.en: The villain devised a fiendish plot.
ex.ko: 그 악당은 극도로 사악한 계획을 꾸몄다.

ex.en: It was a fiendish puzzle to solve.
ex.ko: 그것은 풀기에 지독히 어려운 퍼즐이었다.

fiendishly.en: in an extremely cruel or wicked way

ex.en: He smiled fiendishly before revealing his plan.
ex.ko: 그는 계획을 밝히기 전에 악랄하게 미소지었다.

fiendishly.en: to an extremely great or difficult degree

ex.en: The exam was fiendishly hard.
ex.ko: 그 시험은 지독히 어려웠다.

fiercely.en: in a very strong, violent, or intense way

ex.en: The wind blew fiercely all night.
ex.ko: 밤새 바람이 맹렬하게 불었다.

fiercely.en: with strong feeling, determination, or loyalty

ex.en: She is fiercely loyal to her friends.
ex.ko: 그녀는 친구들에게 대단히 의리가 있다.

firewall.en: a system that protects a computer network from unauthorized access

ex.en: The company installed a firewall to block hackers.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 해커를 막기 위해 방화벽을 설치했다.

firewall.en: a fireproof wall built to stop fire from spreading through a building

ex.en: The firewall between the units slowed the fire's spread.
ex.ko: 그 유닛들 사이의 방화벽이 화재 확산을 늦췄다.

fissure.en: a long, narrow crack or opening in something, especially rock or ground

ex.en: Hot gases escaped through a fissure in the volcano.
ex.ko: 화산의 갈라진 틈에서 뜨거운 가스가 새어 나왔다.

fissure.en: a difference or disagreement that divides people who used to be united

ex.en: A deep fissure has opened up within the party.
ex.ko: 그 정당 내부에 깊은 균열이 생겼다.

fixture.en: a piece of equipment or furniture that is fixed in place, especially in a house

ex.en: The light fixtures in the kitchen need replacing.
ex.ko: 부엌의 조명 설비를 교체해야 한다.

fixture.en: a person or thing that has been present in a particular place or role for so long that it seems permanent

ex.en: He has become a fixture at the local coffee shop.
ex.ko: 그는 동네 커피숍의 단골이 되었다.

fixture.en: (British) a sports match arranged for a particular date

ex.en: Saturday's fixture was postponed due to rain.
ex.ko: 토요일 경기 일정은 비로 인해 연기되었다.
